lede: A look at January 1 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Ian Subiabre and Jason Pierre-Paul, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2011 Alexandria bombing
events.desc: A bomb explodes as Coptic Christians in Alexandria, Egypt, leave a new year service, killing 23 people.

events.title: 2010 Lakki Marwat suicide bombing
events.desc: A suicide car bomber detonates at a volleyball tournament in Lakki Marwat, Pakistan, killing 105 and injuring 100 more.

events.title: Adam Air Flight 574
events.desc: Adam Air Flight 574 breaks apart in mid-air and crashes near the Makassar Strait, Indonesia, killing all 102 people on board.

events.title: Juan Maldacena
events.desc: Argentinian physicist Juan Maldacena publishes a landmark paper initiating the study of AdS/CFT correspondence, which links string theory and quantum gravity.

events.title: World Trade Organization
events.desc: The World Trade Organization comes into being.

events.title: Air India Flight 855
events.desc: Air India Flight 855, a Boeing 747, crashes into the Arabian Sea off the coast of Bombay, India, due to instrument failure, spatial disorientation, and pilot error, killing all 213 people on board.

events.title: People's Democratic Party of Afghanistan
events.desc: The People's Democratic Party of Afghanistan is founded in Kabul, Afghanistan.

events.title: Lèse-majesté in Thailand
events.desc: Lèse majesté in Thailand is strengthened to include "insult" and changed to a crime against national security, after the Thai criminal code of 1956 went into effect.: 6, 18

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The German Luftwaffe launches Operation Bodenplatte, a massive, but failed, attempt to knock out Allied air power in northern Europe in a single blow.

events.title: St. Petersburg–Tampa Airboat Line
events.desc: The SPT Airboat Line becomes the world's first scheduled airline to use a winged aircraft.

deaths.text: Grace Hopper, American computer scientist and admiral, co-developed COBOL
Edwin Lutyens, English architect, designed the Castle Drogo and Thiepval Memorial

observances.text: Christian feast day: Basil the Great (Eastern Orthodox Church)
Christian feast day: Feast of the Circumcision of Christ Feast of the Holy Name of Jesus (Anglican Communion, Lutheran Church)
Christian feast day: Solemnity of Mary, Mother of God; and its related observances: World Day of Peace
The eighth of the Twelve Days of Christmas (Western Christianity)
